title: Add piping from stdin to stdout with a -

issues.body:
I work on typing, too, so I'm wondering what made you choose `Type[]` here and why you chose `io.TextIOBase` specifically. Both choices are wrong here and I will explain why.

1. `Type[]` is to signify the caller is going to pass a *class*, not an *object*. Here we want a regular object.
2. `io.T
This is fine. Also, you can just use `file`. The builtin name `file` is deprecated, we only keep it to not break old code.
I agree this got a bit messy now, let's see suggestions below.
Remove this special case. Dedent the for loop as it was before.
Since `-` is an unlikely real file name, it would be better to say `if not p.is_file() and str(p) == '-':`

Note: with my suggested changes to `main()`, if a user passes more than one file name, the tool will fail with "FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '-'" and I think this is clear enough of an indication that it wasn't treated as stdin by the tool then. Since the report at the end

Ah, I clearly misunderstood the purpose of `Type`. I thught it was to specify an object of a class *or subclass* with the given type. Not running mypy much led to me never finding out I was getting this wrong! :laughing: Will have to read throgh `typing`'s docs a bit more thoroughly.
Removing the report text from stdout when piping looks as simple as indenting this one line in under the else block.
